issue.title: build a app to analyze github requirement and solution
issue.body:
Project Requirements
Build an application that integrates with a local LLM server (no external AI services).
Retrieve issue data (URL + content) using either:

- a specific issue number, or

- issues created/updated today.

Analyze each issue to determine clarity of requirements, including:

- a clarity score

- actionable improvement recommendations

Analyze replies and classify them into:

- Discussion

- Proposed Solution

For replies classified as Proposed Solution:

- estimate technical feasibility (e.g., complexity, effort, risk)

- ignore Discussion replies

Store all results in a readable format (e.g., HTML report or structured output like JSON for further use)
issue.author: bobydo

replies.author: bobydo
replies.body: Love to talk about more details about output format

replies.author: bobydo
replies.body:
Solution
Local LLM integration (no external AI)
All analysis runs through a local Ollama server. The model and endpoint are configured in one place and shared across the entire system.
Fetch issue by number
Given a specific issue number, the system retrieves the full issue â€” title, body, author, metadata, and all comments â€” directly from GitHub.
Fetch today's issues
When no specific issue is specified, the system discovers all issues created or updated today and processes each one automatically.
Clarity scoring
Each issue is evaluated and given a clarity score from 1â€“10, reflecting how well-defined the requirements are.
Improvement recommendations
Alongside the score, the system produces a list of concrete recommendations for making the issue clearer â€” focused on the requirements themselves, not on implementation.
Reply classification
Every reply on an issue is classified as either a Discussion or a Proposed Solution.
Feasibility estimation
Proposed Solution replies are assessed for complexity, effort, and risk, each with a supporting rationale. Discussion replies are skipped entirely.
Reporting
Results are written to a reports folder in any combination of JSON, HTML, and plain text. The HTML report is self-contained with visual indicators for scores and classifications.

feasibility.rationale: The solution requires integrating GitHub API with a local LLM server, processing nested issue data, and implementing classification logic. Key challenges include handling GitHub's rate limits, ensuring accurate reply classification, and generating structured reports. These components involve multiple system interactions with moderate complexity but manageable implementation risks.

recommendations: Define the specific LLM server integration protocol (e.g., API endpoints, communication protocol)
Specify classification criteria for distinguishing Discussion vs Proposed Solution replies
State the target platform (web app, desktop, CLI) and required dependencies
Define success criteria for clarity scoring and feasibility estimation
Include sample input/output examples for expected analysis results
Clarify how to handle pagination/rate limiting when retrieving GitHub issues
clarity.rationale: The requirements show intent but lack critical details about LLM integration, classification rules, platform specifics, and success metrics. Missing acceptance criteria and concrete examples prevent clear implementation guidance.
